Surface idle timeout: marks surface as idle after N seconds of inactivity\n    IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n    def __init__(self, store: SessionStore):\n        self.store = store\n\n    async def route_result(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        origin_surface_id: str | None = None,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> RouteDecision:\n        \"\"\"\n        Determine which surface(s) should receive a result.\n\n        Args:\n            session_id: The session the result belongs to\n            origin_surface_id: The surface where the utterance originated (if known)\n            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n\n        Returns:\n            RouteDecision with target surfaces and routing reason\n        \"\"\"\n        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n\n        # Filter out idle surfaces (but keep always-available ones)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        recently_active = [\n            s for s in active_surfaces\n            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS\n        ]\n\n        # Priority 1: Origin surface (if still connected)\n        if origin_surface_id:\n            for surface in recently_active:\n                if surface[\"id\"] == origin_surface_id and surface[\"state\"] != \"disconnected\":\n                    return RouteDecision(\n                        target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n                        reason=\"origin-surface-active\",\n                    )\n\n        # Priority 2: Most recently active connected surface\n        if recently_active:\n            # Sort by last_seen descending, pick the first non-telegram unless all are telegram\n            recently_active.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n\n            # Prefer non-telegram surfaces for non-critical results\n            if urgency not in (\"critical\", \"high\"):\n                for surface in recently_active:\n                    if surface[\"type\"] != \"telegram\":\n                        return RouteDecision(\n                            target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n                            reason=\"most-recent-active\",\n                        )\n\n            # For high urgency or if only telegram available\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**recently_active[0])],\n                reason=\"most-recent-active-or-fallback\",\n            )\n\n        # Priority 3: Any connected surface (including idle)\n        if active_surfaces:\n            active_surfaces.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**active_surfaces[0])],\n                reason=\"any-connected\",\n            )\n\n        # Priority 4: Always-available fallback (Telegram)\n        fallback = await self.store.get_fallback_surface()\n        if fallback:\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**fallback)],\n                reason=\"always-available-fallback\",\n                fallback_used=True,\n            )\n\n        # No surface available - result will wait in queue\n        return RouteDecision(\n            target_surfaces=[],\n            reason=\"no-surface-available\",\n        )\n\n    async def route_to_all_active(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        exclude_surface_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> list[Surface]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route to all active surfaces (for multi-surface scenarios).\n\n        Used when a user has both canvas and audio surfaces active.\n        \"\"\"\n        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        result = []\n        for s in active_surfaces:\n            if exclude_surface_id and s[\"id\"] == exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:\n                result.append(Surface(**s))\n\n        return result\n\n    async def handle_surface_disconnect(self, surface_id: str, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle a surface disconnecting.\n        Marks surface as disconnected and cleans up if needed.\n        \"\"\"\n        await self.store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n\n    async def handle_surface_reconnect(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle a surface reconnecting after disconnection.\n\n        Returns True if this is a reconnection of an existing surface,\n        False if it's a new surface.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Reconnection - update heartbeat\n                    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n                    return True\n\n        # New surface - register it\n        await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        return False\n\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get workload summary for a session (for surface reconnection).\n        \"\"\"\n        return await self.store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n","numLines":183,"startLine":1,"totalLines":183}}}

Position in beads.jsonl\n54\t        self._processed_beads: set[str] = set()\n55\t\n56\t    async def start(self) -> None:\n57\t        \"\"\"Start the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n58\t        self._running = True\n59\t        self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._watch_loop())\n60\t        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n61\t\n62\t    async def stop(self) -> None:\n63\t        \"\"\"Stop the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n64\t        self._running = False\n65\t        if self._task:\n66\t            self._task.cancel()\n67\t            try:\n68\t                await self._task\n69\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n70\t                pass\n71\t        logger.info(\"Bead watcher stopped\")\n72\t\n73\t    async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n74\t        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events.\"\"\"\n75\t        while self._running:\n76\t            try:\n77\t                await self._check_for_events()\n78\t                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n79\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n80\t                break\n81\t            except Exception as e:\n82\t                logger.error(f\"Error in bead watch loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n83\t                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n84\t\n85\t    async def _check_for_events(self) -> None:\n86\t        \"\"\"Check for new bead events and process them.\"\"\"\n87\t        beads_path = Path(self.BEADS_JSONL)\n88\t        if not beads_path.exists():\n89\t            return\n90\t\n91\t        # Read new lines from beads.jsonl\n92\t        events = await self._read_new_bead_events(beads_path)\n93\t\n94\t        for event in events:\n95\t            await self._process_bead_event(event)\n96\t\n97\t    async def _read_new_bead_events(self, beads_path: Path) -> list[BeadEvent]:\n98\t        \"\"\"Read new bead events from beads.jsonl.\"\"\"\n99\t        events = []\n100\t\n101\t        try:\n102\t            with open(beads_path, \"r\") as f:\n103\t                # Seek to last position\n104\t                f.seek(self._last_position)\n105\t                new_lines = f.readlines()\n106\t\n107\t                # Update position\n108\t                self._last_position = f.tell()\n109\t\n110\t                for line in new_lines:\n111\t                    if not line.strip():\n112\t                        continue\n113\t\n114\t                    try:\n115\t                        bead = json.loads(line)\n116\t                        bead_id = bead.get(\"id\")\n117\t                        status = bead.get(\"status\")\n118\t\n119\t                        if not bead_id or bead_id in self._processed_beads:\n120\t                            continue\n121\t\n122\t                        # Process closed beads\n123\t                        if status == \"closed\":\n124\t                            events.append(BeadEvent(\n125\t                                bead_id=bead_id,\n126\t                                event_type=\"closed\",\n127\t                                timestamp=int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n128\t                                data=bead,\n129\t                            ))\n130\t                            self._processed_beads.add(bead_id)\n131\t\n132\t                    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n133\t                        logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse bead JSON: {e}\")\n134\t\n135\t        except Exception as e:\n136\t            logger.error(f\"Error reading beads file: {e}\")\n137\t\n138\t        return events\n139\t\n140\t    async def _process_bead_event(self, event: BeadEvent) -> None:\n141\t        \"\"\"Process a bead event and route to surfaces.\"\"\"\n142\t        bead_data = event.data\n143\t\n144\t        # Extract metadata\n145\t        metadata = bead_data.get(\"metadata\", {})\n146\t        session_id = metadata.get(\"session_id\")\n147\t        surface_id = metadata.get(\"origin_surface_id\")\n148\t\n149\t        if not session_id:\n150\t            # No session_id - can't route\n151\t            logger.warning(f\"Bead {event.bead_id} has no session_id in metadata\")\n152\t            return\n153\t\n154\t        # Determine target surface\n155\t        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n156\t            session_id=session_id,\n157\t            origin_surface_id=surface_id,\n158\t            urgency=metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n159\t        )\n160\t\n161\t        # Extract result from bead\n162\t        result = await self._extract_result_from_bead(bead_data, session_id)\n163\t\n164\t        if not result:\n165\t            logger.warning(f\"Could not extract result from bead {event.bead_id}\")\n166\t            return\n167\t\n168\t        # Route to target surfaces\n169\t        if decision.target_surfaces:\n170\t            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n171\t                if surface.type == \"telegram\":\n172\t                    await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n173\t                else:\n174\t                    # Fallback to Telegram\n182\t            await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n183\t        else:\n184\t            # No surface available - result stays in queue\n185\t            logger.info(f\"No surface available for bead {event.bead_id}, result queued\")\n186\t\n187\t    async def _extract_result_from_bead(self, bead: dict, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n188\t        \"\"\"Extract result data from a closed bead.\"\"\"\n189\t        # Try to extract structured result from bead body\n190\t        body = bead.get(\"body\", \"\")\n191\t        metadata = bead.get(\"metadata\", {})\n192\t\n193\t        # If metadata already has result structure, use it\n194\t        if \"result\" in metadata:\n195\t            return metadata[\"result\"]\n196\t\n197\t        # Position in beads.jsonl\n        self._processed_beads: set[str] = set()\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._watch_loop())\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._task:\n            self._task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher stopped\")\n\n    async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await self._check_for_events()\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in bead watch loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n\n    async def _check_for_events(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check for new bead events and process them.\"\"\"\n        beads_path = Path(self.BEADS_JSONL)\n        if not beads_path.exists():\n            return\n\n        # Seek to last position\n                f.seek(self._last_position)\n                new_lines = f.readlines()\n\n                # Update position\n                self._last_position = f.tell()\n\n                for line in new_lines:\n                    if not line.strip():\n                        continue\n\n                    try:\n                        bead = json.loads(line)\n                        bead_id = bead.get(\"id\")\n                        status = bead.get(\"status\")\n\n                        if not bead_id or bead_id in self._processed_beads:\n                            continue\n\n                        # Process closed beads\n                        if status == \"closed\":\n                            events.append(BeadEvent(\n                                bead_id=bead_id,\n                                event_type=\"closed\",\n                                timestamp=int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                                data=bead,\n                            ))\n                            self._processed_beads.add(bead_id)\n\n                    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                        logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse bead JSON: {e}\")\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error reading beads file: {e}\")\n\n        return events\n\n    async def _process_bead_event(self, event: BeadEvent) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Process a bead event and route to surfaces.\"\"\"\n        bead_data = event.data\n\n        # No session_id - can't route\n            logger.warning(f\"Bead {event.bead_id} has no session_id in metadata\")\n            return\n\n        # Determine target surface\n        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            origin_surface_id=surface_id,\n            urgency=metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n        )\n\n        # Extract result from bead\n        result = await self._extract_result_from_bead(bead_data, session_id)\n\n        if not result:\n            logger.warning(f\"Could not extract result from bead {event.bead_id}\")\n            return\n\n        # Route to target surfaces\n        if decision.target_surfaces:\n            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n                if surface.type == \"telegram\":\n                    await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n                else:\n                    # Fallback to Telegram\n            await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n        else:\n            # No surface available - result stays in queue\n            logger.info(f\"No surface available for bead {event.bead_id}, result queued\")\n\n    async def _extract_result_from_bead(self, bead: dict, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Extract result data from a closed bead.\"\"\"\n        # Try to extract structured result from bead body\n        body = bead.get(\"body\", \"\")\n        metadata = bead.get(\"metadata\", {})\n\n        # If metadata already has result structure, use it\n        if \"result\" in metadata:\n            return metadata[\"result\"]\n\n        # Try to parse result from body\n        # For now, create a basic result from the bead\n        return {\n            \"id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n            \"type\": \"bead_result\",\n            \"summary\": body[:200] if len(body) > 200 else body,  # Truncate for summary\n            \"data\": {\n                \"bead_id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n                \"title\": bead.get(\"title\"),\n                \"type\": bead.get(\"type\"),\n                \"body\": body,\n                \"status\": bead.get(\"status\"),\n            },\n            \"urgency\": metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n            \"surfaced_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n        }\n\n    async def _send_to_telegram(self, result: dict, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Send result to Telegram via telegram-claude-bridge.\n\n        NOTE: This requires a session→telegram_chat_id mapping. Current implementation\n        logs a warning because telegram-claude-bridge uses a pull-based architecture\n        (manages sessions internally per forum topic) rather than push-based message delivery.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # telegram-claude-bridge proxy expects actual Telegram chat_id (int64), not session_id\n            # Since we don't have a session→chat mapping, log this as unavailable\n            logger.warning(\n                f\"Cannot send result to Telegram for session {session_id}: \"\n                f\"session→telegram_chat mapping not implemented. \"\n                f\"telegram-claude-bridge uses pull-based architecture (per forum topic sessions).\"\n            )\n\n            # Correct contract for reference (if mapping is implemented later):\n            # POST http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000/send\n            # {\n            #   \"chat_id\": 123456789,  # int64, REQUIRED - actual Telegram chat ID\n            #   \"text\": \"message\",     # string, REQUIRED - message content\n            #   \"parse_mode\": \"HTML\"   # string, OPTIONAL\n            # }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error in Telegram send logic: {e}\")\n\n    def _format_telegram_message(self, result: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format result as Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Result available\")\n        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n\n        emoji_map = {\n            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n            \"normal\": \"📌\",\n            \"low\": \"💬\",\n        }\n\n        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"📌\")\n\n        # Build message\n        lines = [\n            f\"{emoji} {summary}\",\n        ]\n\n        # Add detail if available\n        data = result.get(\"data\", {})\n        if \"bead_id\" in data:\n            lines.append(f\"Bead: {data['bead_id']}\")\n\n        return \"\\n\".join(lines)\n\n\nasync def create_bead_watcher(store: SessionStore, router: SurfaceRouter) -> BeadWatcher:\n    \"\"\"Create and start a bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n    watcher = BeadWatcher(store, router)\n    await watcher.start()\n    return watcher\n","numLines":274,"startLine":1,"totalLines":274}}}

Mock the HTTP client to capture payload\n        import httpx\n        captured_payload = {}\n        captured_url = None\n\n        class MockResponse:\n            status_code = 200\n\n        async def mock_post(url, json, timeout):\n            nonlocal captured_payload, captured_url\n            captured_url = url\n            captured_payload = json\n            return MockResponse()\n\n        # Reset global instance\n        import src.telegram.fallback\n        src.telegram.fallback._telegram_fallback = None\n\n        fallback = get_telegram_fallback()\n        assert fallback is not None\n        assert isinstance(fallback, TelegramFallback)\n\n    def test_get_telegram_fallback_returns_same_instance(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that get_telegram_fallback returns singleton instance.\"\"\"\n        # Reset global instance\n        import src.telegram.fallback\n        src.telegram.fallback._telegram_fallback = None\n\n        fallback1 = get_telegram_fallback()\n        fallback2 = get_telegram_fallback()\n        assert fallback1 is fallback2\n\n    def test_get_telegram_fallback_uses_env_var(self, monkeypatch):\n        \"\"\"Test that get_telegram_fallback respects ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL env var.\"\"\"\n        # Reset global instance\n        import src.telegram.fallback\n        src.telegram.fallback._telegram_fallback = None\n\n        test_url = \"http://test-bridge:9999\"\n        monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_TELEGRAM_BRIDGE_URL\", test_url)\n\n        fallback = get_telegram_fallback()\n        assert fallback.bridge_url == test_url\n\n\nclass TestRateLimiting:\n    \"\"\"Test failure log rate limiting to prevent spam.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_first_failure_logs_warning(self, caplog):\n        \"\"\"Test that the first failure logs a WARNING.\"\"\"\n        fallback = TelegramFallback(bridge_url=\"http://test-bridge:8000\")\n\n        with caplog.at_level(\"WARNING\"):\n            fallback._handle_send_failure(\"test error\")\n\n        assert len(caplog.records) == 1\n        assert caplog.records[0].levelname == \"WARNING\"\n        assert \"Telegram send failure #1\" in caplog.records[0].message\n        assert \"test error\" in caplog.records[0].message\n\n    def test_immediate_repeated_failure_logs_debug(self, caplog):\n        \"\"\"Test that immediate repeated failures log at DEBUG level.\"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime, timedelta\n\n        fallback = TelegramFallback(bridge_url=\"http://test-bridge:8000\")\n\n        # First failure logs WARNING\n        with caplog.at_level(\"WARNING\"):\n            fallback._handle_send_failure(\"first error\")\n\n        # Clear previous logs\n        caplog.clear()\n\n        # Second failure within cooldown should log at DEBUG level\n        with caplog.at_level(\"DEBUG\"):\n            fallback._handle_send_failure(\"second error\")\n\n        # Should have a DEBUG log for the repeated failure\n        debug_records = [r for r in caplog.records if r.levelname == \"DEBUG\"]\n        warning_records = [r for r in caplog.records if r.levelname == \"WARNING\"]\n\n        assert len(warning_records) == 0, \"Repeated failure should not log at WARNING level\"\n        assert len(debug_records) >= 1, \"Repeated failure should log at DEBUG level\"\n        assert any(\"Repeated Telegram send failure\" in r.message for r in debug_records)\n\n    def test_failure_after_cooldown_logs_warning(self, caplog):\n        \"\"\"Test that a failure after the cooldown period logs a WARNING again.\"\"\"\n        from datetime import datetime, timedelta\n        from unittest.mock import patch\n\n        fallback = TelegramFallback(bridge_url=\"http://test-bridge:8000\")\n\n        # First failure\n        with caplog.at_level(\"WARNING\"):\n            fallback._handle_send_failure(\"first error\")\n\n        # Clear previous logs\n        caplog.clear()\n\n        # Mock time to be after cooldown period (5 minutes + 1 second)\n        future_time = datetime.now() + timedelta(seconds=301)\n        with patch('src.telegram.fallback.datetime') as mock_datetime:\n            mock_datetime.now.return_value = future_time\n            with caplog.at_level(\"WARNING\"):\n                fallback._handle_send_failure(\"second error\")\n\n        # file changes without restart.\"\"\"\n        # Initial load\n        config = reload_manager.get_config('exceptions')\n        assert config['categories']['blocking']['no_canvas_timeout_minutes'] == 10\n\n        # Modify the file\n        import yaml\n        with open(temp_exceptions_yaml, 'w') as f:\n            new_config = config.copy()\n @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_hot_reload_check_interval(self, reload_manager, temp_exceptions_yaml):\n        \"\"\"Test that hot-reload respects check interval by preventing rapid re-reads.\"\"\"\n        import yaml\n\n        # Get the initial mtime\n        initial_mtime = reload_manager.get_mtime('exceptions')\n\n        # Read the config (this updates last_check time)\n        _ = reload_manager.get_config('exceptions')\n\n        # Immediately check again - should NOT reload (within check interval)\n        reloaded = reload_manager._check_and_reload('exceptions')\n        assert reloaded == False, \"Should not reload within check interval if file unchanged\"\n\n        # Modify the file\n        with open(temp_exceptions_yaml, 'w') as f:\n            config = reload_manager.get_config('exceptions')\n            yaml.dump(config, f)\n\n        # Check again immediately - still should not reload (within interval)\n        reloaded = reload_manager._check_and_reload('exceptions')\n        assert reloaded == False, \"Should not reload within check interval even if file changed\"\n\n        # Force reload to bypass the interval\n Line 1471-1479\n**Purpose:** API endpoint to get Telegram bridge status\n\n**Current error handling:**\n- Catches general `Exception`\n- Returns error response if status check fails\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling Pattern Summary\n\nAll Telegram send failures follow this pattern (implemented in `_handle_send_failure()`):\n\n1. **First failure after startup:** Logs at WARNING level with details\n2. **Subsequent failures:** Logs at DEBUG level only to avoid spam\n3. **Failure tracking:** Maintains `_failure_count` and `_has_logged_first_failure` state\n4. **Reachability state:** Sets `_is_reachable = False` on failure\n\n---\n\n## Key Findings\n\n1. **Fully implemented:** `send_message()` and `send_result()` are the only fully functional send operations\n2. **Not implemented:** `send_exception()` and `send_workload_summary()` are stubs that return `False`\n3. **Bead watcher send:** `_send_to_telegram()` currently just logs a warning\n4. **Architecture mismatch:** telegram-claude-bridge uses pull-based architecture (manages sessions internally per forum topic), while adc expects push-based delivery\n5. **Rate limiting:** Failure logs are rate-limited to avoid spam (first failure: WARNING, subsequent: DEBUG)\n\n---\n\n## Contract Reference (telegram-claude-bridge)\n\n**Send endpoint:** `POST http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000/send`\n\n**Request body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"chat_id\": 123456789,     // int64, REQUIRED - Line 1471-1479\n**Purpose:** API endpoint to get Telegram bridge status\n\n**Current error handling:**\n- Catches general `Exception`\n- Returns error response if status check fails\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling Pattern Summary\n\nAll Telegram send failures follow this pattern (implemented in `_handle_send_failure()`):\n\n1. **First failure after startup:** Logs at WARNING level with details\n2. **Subsequent failures:** Logs at DEBUG level only to avoid spam\n3. **Failure tracking:** Maintains `_failure_count` and `_has_logged_first_failure` state\n4. **Reachability state:** Sets `_is_reachable = False` on failure\n\n---\n\n## Key Findings\n\n1. **Fully implemented:** `send_message()` and `send_result()` are the only fully functional send operations\n2. **Not implemented:** `send_exception()` and `send_workload_summary()` are stubs that return `False`\n3. **Bead watcher send:** `_send_to_telegram()` currently just logs a warning\n4. **Architecture mismatch:** telegram-claude-bridge uses pull-based architecture (manages sessions internally per forum topic), while adc expects push-based delivery\n5. **Rate limiting:** Failure logs are rate-limited to avoid spam (first failure: WARNING, subsequent: DEBUG)\n\n---\n\n## Contract Reference (telegram-claude-bridge)\n\n**Send endpoint:** `POST http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000/send`\n\n**Request body:**\n```json\n{\n  \"chat_id\": 123456789,     // int64, REQUIRED -
